C 78

MELBOURNE SEAFOOD STATION

This grade is calculated by us from Florida DBPR public inspection records — Florida does not issue official grades. How we calculate it →

Data last updated: February 20, 2026

C · 78/100 · safer than 15% of Brevard County restaurants we grade

Middle of the road for MELBOURNE SEAFOOD STATION: a C (78/100) at its last health inspection on February 20, 2026.

Inspectors cited 4 intermediate and 10 basic violations. That puts it safer than 15% of Brevard County restaurants we grade. Diner rating: 4.5 from 2,432 reviews. Menus here flag gluten-free, dairy-free, keto, paleo, and other dietary options.

Get directions Call (321) 327-3393

Inspection history

The following records are from Florida DBPR public inspection records, most recent first.

  1. February 20, 2026 · Routine - Food

    Inspection Completed - No Further Action

    0 high-priority · 4 intermediate · 10 basic

    • 02 — Consumer advisory, date-marking & labeling of ready-to-eat foods (×2)
    • 08 — Cross-contamination: raw foods separated; food protected during prep/storage (×2)
    • 10 — In-use food dispensing utensils properly stored (×2)
    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained
    • 16 — Dishwashing facilities; sanitizer test kits; gauges
    • 23 — Nonfood-contact surfaces clean
    • 25 — Single-service and single-use items (×2)
    • 31 — Handwash sinks accessible; handwashing supplies & sign provided
    • 32 — Bathrooms
    • 53 — Food manager certification; employee food-safety training

  2. January 26, 2026 · Routine - Food

    Call Back - Complied

    0 high-priority · 0 intermediate · 0 basic

    No violations cited.

  3. December 22, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Call Back - Extension given, pending

    0 high-priority · 1 intermediate · 0 basic

    • 53 — Food manager certification; employee food-safety training

  4. December 12, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Call Back - Extension given, pending

    0 high-priority · 5 intermediate · 2 basic

    • 01 — Food from approved source; safe, unadulterated, sound condition (×2)
    • 11 — Employee health knowledge; no ill/symptomatic employee present
    • 23 — Nonfood-contact surfaces clean
    • 50 — Current license, properly displayed
    • 53 — Food manager certification; employee food-safety training (×2)

  5. October 13, 2025 · Routine - Food

    Warning Issued

    0 high-priority · 6 intermediate · 8 basic

    • 01 — Food from approved source; safe, unadulterated, sound condition (×2)
    • 08 — Cross-contamination: raw foods separated; food protected during prep/storage
    • 11 — Employee health knowledge; no ill/symptomatic employee present (×2)
    • 12 — Handwashing; employee eating/drinking/smoking practices
    • 14 — Food-contact & nonfood-contact surfaces properly designed/maintained
    • 16 — Dishwashing facilities; sanitizer test kits; gauges
    • 23 — Nonfood-contact surfaces clean
    • 24 — Storage & handling of clean equipment and utensils; air drying
    • 25 — Single-service and single-use items
    • 50 — Current license, properly displayed
    • 53 — Food manager certification; employee food-safety training (×2)
